practice.name: Problem 4 (2001 AIME I)
practice.statement: In triangle ABC, angles A and B measure 60° and 80°, respectively. The bisector of angle C intersects AB at D, and AD = 1. The area of triangle ABC can be written in the form (a√b)/c, where a, b, and c are positive integers, and b is not divisible by the square of any prime. Find a + b + c.

practice.name: Problem 19 (2019 AMC 12A)
practice.statement: In triangle ABC with integer side lengths, cos A = 7/9. What is the least possible perimeter for ABC?

practice.name: Problem 13 (2018 AIME I)
practice.statement: Let triangle ABC have side lengths AB = 13, BC = 14, and CA = 15. Point P lies in the interior of triangle ABC, and points D and E are the incenters of triangle ABP and triangle ACP, respectively. Find the minimum possible area of triangle ADE as P varies along BC.
